categories.name: Element Registry
categories.description: Primitive 1 — Register and query annotated UI elements by semantic probe ID.

scenarios.name: Query existing element returns full ProbeElement
scenarios.given: A page with <table data-probe-id='order-list' data-probe-type='data-container' data-probe-state='loaded'> rendered and the probe collector injected
scenarios.then: Returns a ProbeElement with id='order-list', type='data-container', state.current='loaded', layout.visible=true, layout.width > 0

scenarios.name: Query non-existent element returns null
scenarios.given: A page with no element having probe ID 'missing-widget'
scenarios.then: Returns null without throwing

features.name: Query all elements filtered by type

scenarios.name: queryAll with type filter returns only matching elements
scenarios.given: A page with 3 action-type, 2 form-type, and 1 data-container-type annotated elements registered
scenarios.then: Returns an array of exactly 3 ProbeElement objects all having type='action'

scenarios.name: queryPage returns page summary including unready elements
scenarios.given: A page with a <div data-probe-id='orders-page' data-probe-type='page' data-probe-state='loading'> and two child elements still loading
scenarios.then: Returns { id: 'orders-page', state: 'loading', elements: [...all elements], unreadyElements: ['order-list', 'filter-bar'] } where unreadyElements contains the IDs of still-loading elements

categories.name: State Exposure
categories.description: Primitive 2 — Expose real-time element state as structured data for assertion without screenshot inspection.

scenarios.name: getState returns current state after element is loaded
scenarios.given: A page with <div data-probe-id='order-list' data-probe-state='loaded'> and the probe collector active
scenarios.then: Returns a StateInfo object with current='loaded', timestamp set to a recent epoch millisecond value

scenarios.name: getStates returns batch state map
scenarios.given: A page with 3 annotated elements: 'order-list' (loaded), 'status-filter' (idle), 'submit-btn' (disabled)
scenarios.then: Returns { 'order-list': { current: 'loaded', ... }, 'status-filter': { current: 'idle', ... }, 'submit-btn': { current: 'disabled', ... } } with all three entries populated

categories.name: Event Stream
categories.description: Primitive 3 — Event-driven waits and subscriptions instead of polling or fixed timeouts.

scenarios.name: waitForState resolves when element transitions to target state
scenarios.given: An element 'order-list' currently in state 'loading'. The application updates data-probe-state to 'loaded' after the API call completes 300ms later.
scenarios.then: The promise resolves within ~300ms (event-driven, not polling) without waiting for any fixed timeout

scenarios.name: waitForState times out when state never reached
scenarios.given: An element 'broken-list' whose data-probe-state attribute is never updated by the application
scenarios.then: The promise rejects after 2000ms with Error: "waitForState('broken-list', 'loaded') timed out after 2000ms"

scenarios.name: waitForPageReady resolves when page element reaches loaded
scenarios.given: A page with <div data-probe-id='orders-page' data-probe-type='page' data-probe-state='loading'>. The application updates state to 'loaded' after initial data fetch completes.
scenarios.then: The promise resolves once data-probe-state changes to 'loaded', event-driven with no polling delay

scenarios.name: waitForPageReady times out listing still-loading elements
scenarios.given: A page where 'order-list' remains in state 'loading' because the API call is hanging
scenarios.then: The promise rejects with Error: "waitForPageReady timed out. Still loading: order-list" after 5000ms

scenarios.name: onStateChange callback fires on every state transition
scenarios.given: An element 'submit-btn' registered with the probe collector
scenarios.when: probe.onStateChange('submit-btn', (oldState, newState) => transitions.push({ oldState, newState })) is called, and the button transitions from 'idle' → 'submitting' → 'idle'
scenarios.then: The callback fires twice. transitions[0] = { oldState: 'idle', newState: 'submitting' }, transitions[1] = { oldState: 'submitting', newState: 'idle' }. The returned unsubscribe function cleans up the listener.

categories.name: Source Binding
categories.description: Primitive 4 — Link UI elements to their API data sources and wait for network requests to complete.

features.name: Wait for source binding after API call

scenarios.name: waitForSource resolves after matching network request completes
scenarios.given: A data table annotated with data-probe-source='GET /api/orders'. The page has just loaded and the GET /api/orders request is in-flight.
scenarios.then: Resolves with SourceInfo: { url: '/api/orders', method: 'GET', status: 200, responseTime: <ms> } once the request completes. The element's source field is populated.

scenarios.name: getNetworkLog returns all intercepted requests with element correlation
scenarios.given: Two API calls made after page load: GET /api/orders (matched to 'order-list') and GET /api/users (not matched to any element)
scenarios.when: probe.getNetworkLog() is called after both requests complete
scenarios.then: Returns an array of 2 NetworkLogEntry objects. The first has elementId='order-list'. The second has elementId=undefined.

categories.name: Layout Metrics
categories.description: Primitive 5 — Position, size, visibility, scroll, and render timing from the rendering engine.

scenarios.name: getLayout returns position and size metrics
scenarios.given: A button element 'submit-btn' rendered at pixel position (100, 200) with dimensions 120×40 and visible in the viewport
scenarios.then: Returns LayoutInfo with x=100, y=200, width=120, height=40, visible=true

scenarios.name: getOverlaps detects overlapping probe elements
scenarios.given: A page where a tooltip element 'error-tooltip' overlaps the 'submit-btn' element by a 50×20 pixel area
scenarios.then: Returns [{ a: 'submit-btn', b: 'error-tooltip', overlapArea: 1000 }] where overlapArea=1000 (50*20)
scenarios.platform_note: Web only — getOverlaps is not implemented on Flutter, iOS, Android, Windows, or React Native

categories.name: Action Dispatch
categories.description: Primitive 6 — Semantic action dispatch with 5-point pre-checks and automatic linkage verification.

scenarios.name: click dispatches real DOM event on a visible enabled element
scenarios.given: A button <button data-probe-id='submit-btn' data-probe-state='idle'> that is visible and enabled. A click listener sets a clickedFlag.
scenarios.then: The real DOM click event fires, clickedFlag is true. No ProbeActionError thrown.

scenarios.name: fill sets React controlled input value correctly
scenarios.given: A React controlled input <input data-probe-id='search-box' value={searchTerm}> with an onChange handler that updates state
scenarios.then: The native value setter + input + change events trigger React's onChange, resulting in the input displaying 'ORDER-123' and the React state being updated

scenarios.name: select succeeds with valid option
scenarios.given: A dropdown <select data-probe-id='status-filter' data-probe-type='selector'> with data.options=['all', 'pending', 'completed', 'cancelled'] in state 'idle'
scenarios.then: The change event fires with value='completed'. The element's data.value is updated to 'completed'. No ProbeActionError thrown.

categories.name: Action Dispatch — Pre-Check Errors
categories.description: The 5-point pre-check sequence throws structured ProbeActionError objects for machine-readable failure diagnosis.

scenarios.name: click on unregistered element throws NOT_FOUND
scenarios.given: A page with no element having probe ID 'delete-btn' (annotation missing or element not in DOM)
scenarios.then: Throws ProbeActionError: { error: 'NOT_FOUND', id: 'delete-btn' }. The error object is a plain JS object, not an Error instance.

scenarios.name: click on hidden element throws NOT_VISIBLE
scenarios.given: A button 'export-btn' registered in the probe registry with layout.visible=false (scrolled out of viewport or hidden by CSS)
scenarios.then: Throws ProbeActionError: { error: 'NOT_VISIBLE', id: 'export-btn' } before any DOM event is dispatched

scenarios.name: click on disabled element throws DISABLED with reason
scenarios.given: A submit button 'submit-order-btn' with state.current='disabled' and state.validationErrors=[{ field: 'quantity', message: 'Required' }]
scenarios.then: Throws ProbeActionError: { error: 'DISABLED', id: 'submit-order-btn', reason: 'quantity: Required' } where reason concatenates the validation error messages

scenarios.name: click on loading element throws BUSY
scenarios.given: A button 'refresh-btn' whose state.current='loading' because a previous click is still in-flight
scenarios.then: Throws ProbeActionError: { error: 'BUSY', id: 'refresh-btn', reason: 'Element is loading' } without dispatching a new click event

scenarios.name: select with invalid value throws OPTION_NOT_FOUND with available list
scenarios.given: A dropdown 'status-filter' with data.options=['all', 'pending', 'completed'] in state 'idle'
scenarios.then: Throws ProbeActionError: { error: 'OPTION_NOT_FOUND', id: 'status-filter', available: ['all', 'pending', 'completed'] } before any DOM event is dispatched

categories.name: Composite Operations
categories.description: High-level composite operations: actAndWait combines action + state wait + automatic linkage verification; verifyLinkage validates data flow chains explicitly.

scenarios.name: actAndWait selects option, waits for target state, auto-verifies linkage
scenarios.given: A dropdown 'status-filter' with data-probe-linkage='[{"id":"order-list","effect":"data_reload","path":{"type":"api","url":"/api/orders"}}]'. Selecting 'completed' triggers a GET /api/orders?status=completed call which reloads the table.
scenarios.then: Returns ActAndWaitResult with actionDuration (ms to dispatch select), waitDuration (ms to reach 'loaded'), targetState.current='loaded', and linkageResults showing directEffects[0].target='order-list', result='pass', plus apiCalls[0].url='/api/orders' with status=200

scenarios.name: actAndWait returns timeout linkage result when target does not respond
scenarios.given: A 'status-filter' element with a linkage declaration to 'order-list', but the order-list element never emits a state-change event because the data flow is broken
scenarios.when: probe.actAndWait('status-filter', 'select:completed', { target: 'order-list', state: 'loaded', timeout: 5000 }) is awaited
scenarios.then: The actAndWait wait for target state resolves or times out, and linkageResults.directEffects[0].result='timeout'. The method does not throw — timeout is surfaced in the result, not as an exception.

scenarios.name: verifyLinkage validates multi-target cascade linkage chain
scenarios.given: A province selector 'province-select' with linkage targets: [{id:'city-select', effect:'options_update', path:{type:'api', url:'/api/cities'}}, {id:'district-select', effect:'options_update', path:{type:'chain', through:'city-select'}}]. Selecting a province triggers an API call that updates city options, which in turn triggers district options update.
scenarios.when: probe.select('province-select', 'Guangdong') is awaited, then probe.verifyLinkage('province-select', 'select:Guangdong') is awaited
scenarios.then: Returns LinkageResult with directEffects=[{target:'city-select', effect:'options_update', result:'pass', duration:<ms>}], chainedEffects=[{target:'district-select', effect:'options_update', through:'city-select', result:'pass', duration:<ms>}], apiCalls=[{url:'/api/cities', status:200, ...}]

categories.name: Snapshot and Diff
categories.description: Point-in-time state capture and field-level comparison for regression baseline testing.

scenarios.name: snapshot captures all element states at a point in time
scenarios.given: A page with 5 annotated elements all in loaded/idle state
scenarios.when: const baseline = probe.snapshot() is called after page load
scenarios.then: Returns ProbeSnapshot with timestamp (epoch ms), elements (Record keyed by probe ID containing all 5 ProbeElements), and platformContext reflecting the current device/viewport

scenarios.name: diff returns field-level changes between two snapshots
scenarios.given: A baseline snapshot where order-list has data.rows=10 and state.current='loaded'. After filtering, a second snapshot where order-list has data.rows=3 and state.current='loaded'.
scenarios.then: Returns [{ elementId: 'order-list', field: 'data.rows', before: 10, after: 3 }]. Only changed fields appear in the diff.

categories.name: Hierarchy — Effective Visibility
categories.description: Ancestor-chain visibility checks. isEffectivelyVisible() walks the parent chain so a visible child inside a hidden container correctly reports as not visible.

scenarios.name: isEffectivelyVisible returns false when ancestor is hidden
scenarios.given: A panel 'advanced-filters' with layout.visible=false. A child input 'date-range-input' inside the panel with its own layout.visible=true (the element itself is not hidden, but its parent panel is collapsed).
scenarios.then: Returns false — because getAncestorChain('date-range-input') includes 'advanced-filters' which has visible=false, so the full chain check fails

scenarios.name: queryChildren returns direct children of a container
scenarios.given: A 'filter-toolbar' element with children=['status-filter', 'date-range', 'search-box'] in its probe annotation
scenarios.then: Returns an array of 3 ProbeElement objects with ids ['status-filter', 'date-range', 'search-box'], resolved from the registry

categories.name: Platform Context — Device Matrix Testing
categories.description: Apply named device presets and run tests across multiple device profiles for responsive layout and behavior verification.

features.name: Run test across multiple device profiles

scenarios.name: runAcrossDevices executes test on each device and returns per-device results
scenarios.given: A responsive order page that should work on mobile and desktop. The test checks that 'order-list' is visible and 'mobile-menu-btn' is only visible on phone form factor.
scenarios.when: probe.runAcrossDevices(['iphone-15-pro', 'desktop-1080p'], async (p) => { const list = p.query('order-list'); assert(list.layout.visible); }) is awaited
scenarios.then: Returns { 'iphone-15-pro': { device: 'iphone-15-pro', passed: true, duration: <ms> }, 'desktop-1080p': { device: 'desktop-1080p', passed: true, duration: <ms> } }. Each device runs sequentially with setDevice() called before the test function.
